title: Search a Sorted Matrix
description:
Given an `m × n` matrix where each row is sorted ascending AND the first integer of each row is greater than the last integer of the previous row (so the flattened matrix is sorted), return true if target is in the matrix.

**The point**: a row-by-row linear scan is O(m·n). Treating the matrix as one big sorted array and doing binary search gives **O(log(m·n))** — for a 1000×1000 matrix, that's ~20 comparisons vs a million.
hints: Don't search row by row — that throws away half the constraint.
Because the matrix is 'fully sorted in row-major order', you can pretend it's a single sorted array of length m·n.
Convert flat index k → row = k/n, col = k%n. Binary search over k in 0..m·n-1.
